name: CrowdStrike
roast: The Falcon agent living in your kernel is genuinely hard engineering. The $50/endpoint/month pricing for what increasingly looks like a fancy log forwarder to an AI summarizer? That part's on borrowed time. Hope you enjoyed that worldwide BSOD dress rehearsal.

name: Yubico
roast: Congratulations on being one of the few companies at RSA whose moat requires a semiconductor fab to cross. No amount of vibe coding is going to 3D-print a secure element. You're safe -- until someone figures out how to ship a YubiKey as a VS Code extension.

name: PowerDMARC
roast: Your entire product is calling DNS TXT records and parsing them against RFCs that haven't changed since 2014. The 'Early Stage Expo' booth is appropriate -- this business should have stayed at the early stage and pivoted.
cursor_prompt: Build a full-stack DMARC/SPF/DKIM monitoring platform. Use Next.js 14 + Tailwind for the dashboard UI, Postgres for storing domain configs and report history. Core engine: 1) DNS TXT record fetcher using `dns.resolveTxt()` to pull SPF/DKIM/DMARC records, 2) DMARC aggregate report (RUA) parser -- these are just XML files sent to a mailbox, parse with `fast-xml-parser`, 3) SPF record validator (recursive include/redirect resolution, mechanism evaluation per RFC 7208), 4) DKIM selector enumeration and public key verification, 5) MTA-STS policy fetcher (it's a .well-known text file), 6) TLS-RPT JSON report parser. Add a cron job that re-checks domains every 24h and diffs results. Dashboard shows pass/fail per domain, aggregate DMARC report visualization (source IPs, pass rates), and alerts on misconfigurations. The entire protocol suite is public RFCs with zero ambiguity. Ship it in a weekend.

name: Snyk
roast: You raised $1B+ to grep node_modules for CVEs. Your SAST is pattern-matching ASTs, your SCA is a lookup table against public vulnerability databases, and now you slapped 'AI Trust Platform' on the homepage because the VCs need an exit story. Claude already understands code semantics better than your rules engine ever will.
cursor_prompt: Build a developer security scanning platform. Tech stack: TypeScript CLI + Next.js dashboard + Postgres. For SCA: parse lockfiles (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, requirements.txt, go.sum, Cargo.lock) to build a dependency tree, then query the free OSV.dev API for each package/version to get known vulns. For SAST: use tree-sitter to parse source code into ASTs for JS/TS/Python/Go/Java, then write detection rules for OWASP Top 10 patterns. For container scanning: parse Dockerfiles, extract base image, run SCA on OS packages inside image layers. For IaC: parse Terraform/CloudFormation as HCL/JSON/YAML, check against CIS benchmark rules. Wire into a GitHub App that comments on PRs with findings. Use OpenAI API for human-readable fix suggestions. Store results in Postgres, dashboard shows trends over time per repo.

name: Cyble
roast: Your homepage says 'AI-Native' twice above the fold. Your 'cutting-edge cyber intelligence' is an LLM summarizing dark web paste dumps that anyone with a Tor browser and a ChatGPT subscription could replicate during a lunch break. The 'real-time risk insights' are vibes.

name: BotCity
roast: You invented the term 'Shadow Python' so you could sell a solution to a problem nobody was Googling. Your product is `pip list` with a compliance dashboard. The RSA booth fee probably cost more than it would take to rebuild this as a pre-commit hook.
cursor_prompt: Build a Python runtime governance tool. CLI agent that scans environments for Python installations and virtual environments, runs `pip list --format=json` to build inventory, compares against an allowlist policy (YAML config). Ship a Next.js dashboard showing inventory per host, flags policy violations, tracks drift over time. Add a GitHub Action blocking PRs adding unapproved dependencies.

name: Airia
roast: You 'eliminate AI anxiety' by selling an AI governance platform -- which is itself AI. It's AI all the way down. Your product is a permission layer and audit log over LLM API calls, which is approximately what an API gateway with a logging middleware already does. Peak 2025 energy at a 2026 conference.

name: Swissbit
roast: You make actual silicon in actual factories in actual Switzerland. Your biggest risk isn't an AI startup -- it's a tariff negotiation. The only thing a vibe coder can do to compete with you is order your chips on Mouser and put them in a worse enclosure.

name: Insane Cyber
roast: You ship a literal pelican case full of hardware into oil rigs and power plants. Your moat is that your customers don't have Wi-Fi, let alone a GitHub Copilot subscription. The 'Cygnet Flyaway Kit' sounds like a DnD item but it's unkillable by software alone. Respect.

name: Varonis
roast: Your core product -- 'who has access to what file and should they' -- is a graph traversal query that gets harder at enterprise scale. The data classification engine is basically regex + ML that an LLM now does better out of the box. You're surviving on the sheer pain of crawling petabytes of unstructured SharePoint hellscapes that nobody else wants to touch. That's a moat, but it's a moat made of suffering.
